nativeRange.habitat: Still or slow-moving shallow freshwater habitats — ponds, lakes, marshes, and sluggish streams in subtropical to tropical lowlands.

decorPreferences: nutrient-rich aquatic soil for root anchor
open surface area for floating leaves and flowers

goodWith: peaceful community fish that do not uproot plants
avoidWith: vegetarian fish that shred floating leaves

summary: Tropical floating-leaf plant from the Americas with glossy round leaves and short-lived yellow three-petaled flowers; generally considered a pond and paludarium specialist rather than a typical aquarium plant.
careNotes: Hydrocleys nymphoides is generally advised for ponds, paludariums, and large open-top setups rather than conventional aquariums — leaves and flowers sit on or just above the water surface, so it has limited use in covered glass tanks. Tends to perform best when rooted in nutrient-rich aquatic soil in shallow water (commonly cited 10–40 cm depth), with strong overhead lighting (full sun outdoors, or bright full-spectrum LED indoors). Reported to spread 1–1.5 m horizontally via stolons; regular trimming is generally advised to manage runaway growth and to keep the surface open for other plants. Yellow flowers with darker centers are short-lived (often one day each) but bloom continuously through warm months when water is around 21 °C or warmer. Not frost-hardy — has been reported to tolerate brief dips toward 4 °C but should be overwintered indoors in temperate climates. Considered invasive in parts of Australia, New Zealand, southern Africa, and the southern United States, so should not be released into local waterways. Sources cross-checked: Wikipedia (taxonomy and range), Buce Plant (care level and propagation), Pond Informer (size and depth tolerance); Buce Plant secondary URL from the dispatch (buceplant.com root) returned 404, so the actual product page at buceplant.com/products/hydrocleys-nymphoides-water-poppy was used instead.
